ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Aug-28-2020 Continued To Oct-27-20 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Defendant Safeway Services Gp, Inc.'S Demurrer To Complaint [Stipulated Continuance From April 8, 2020; Briefing Schedule To Track New Hearing Date; Email Dated 7/23/2020]
Off Calendar Aug-27-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant Safeway Services Gp, Inc.'S Demurrer To Complaint.

Defendant Safeway Service G.P., Inc's demurrer to complaint is sustained without leave to amend as to the "cause of action" for gross negligence. The law does not recognize an independent cause of action for gross negligence under the instant circumstances. (See Saenz v. Whitewater Voyages, Inc. (1990) 226 Cal.App.3d 758, 766, fn. 9 ["In reality, California does not recognize a distinct cause of action for 'gross negligence' independent of a statutory basis."].) Moreover, the gross negligence allegations are unnecessary because plaintiff is not attempting to avoid a waiver/release of liability. (Cf. Eriksson v. Nunick (2011) 191 Cal.App.4th 826, 856 [defendant moving for summary judgment based on a release needed to present evidence that it did not act with gross negligence because complaint contained allegations of gross negligence]; Chavez v. 24 Hour Fitness USA, Inc. (2015) 238 Cal.App.4th 632 [discussing gross negligence in the context of a release]; Rosencrans v. Dover Images, Ltd (2011) 192 Cal.App.4th1072 [same].)

The demurrer to the "cause of action" for punitive damages is moot because that issue is addressed in the motion to strike.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, NOT Reported. =(302/EPS)
